response.text:
# REFLECTION

Household r0c4 is in critical islanded condition with SoC at 40.8% (15.09/37 kWh) and declining under sustained 1.0 kW load, with no grid connection and no inbound supply available. Over the past two hours, r0c4 has received repeated urgent requests from three neighbors (r0c3, r0c5, r1c4)—all reporting SoC fractions 10% below their share thresholds—yet r0c4 itself has been rejected by all three peers citing their own critical reserves and islanded stress. The pattern indicates network-wide energy scarcity with no viable redistribution path; r0c4's own survival horizon is approximately 15 hours at current load if no grid reconnection occurs. Sharing any energy now would accelerate r0c4's descent toward critical depletion (below 20% SoC), risking load shedding or complete blackout. The most prudent stance is to enter strict conservation mode: reject all outbound requests regardless of urgency, minimize internal load where possible, and preserve reserves for essential household functions until either grid reconnection restores supply or SoC stabilizes above 55%.

```yaml
sharing_intent: conservative
share_min_soc_frac: 0.55
max_share_kw_per_tick: 0.0
recipient_priority: []
distrusted_peers: []
request_urgency: urgent
belief_note: "Islanded, SoC declining, network-wide stress. All peers also critical. No viable inbound supply. Self-preservation mode: zero outbound sharing until SoC recovery above 55% or grid reconnection."
ttl_ticks: 60
```

system: You are the planning subroutine of a household energy-coordination agent. Given recent state, beliefs, and trust circles, output (1) a one-paragraph REFLECTION on what you've observed (just text), and (2) a POLICY in a YAML code-fence — sharing_intent (conservative|balanced|generous), share_min_soc_frac (0..1), max_share_kw_per_tick (kW), recipient_priority (list of {circle, weight}), distrusted_peers (list of house ids), request_urgency (low|normal|urgent), belief_note (string), ttl_ticks (int >= 1).
